proposed_hypotheses.text: The six treatment indicators (treatment_enzalutamide, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab) differ in their unadjusted association with objective_response, and at least one treatment shows a non-trivial main effect on objective_response.
Patients commonly receive more than one of the six treatments, so unadjusted treatment-response comparisons are confounded by co-treatment.

analyses.code: df.groupby(t)['objective_response'].mean() for each treatment t; df['n_treatments'] = df[treatment_cols].sum(axis=1); value_counts.
analyses.result_summary: Overall response rate 24.0% (12,011/50,000). Unadjusted treatment-vs-no-treatment response-rate differences: enzalutamide +20.2 pp (36.1% on vs 15.9% off), abiraterone -0.3 pp, docetaxel -0.0 pp, olaparib -0.1 pp, lu177_psma +0.3 pp, pembrolizumab -0.2 pp. Patients commonly receive multiple treatments: 21% none, 40% one, 28% two, 9% three, 1.5% four, ~0.1% five, 1 patient six. Co-occurrence of all pairs is non-trivial.

proposed_hypotheses.text: mcrpc=1 (metastatic castration-resistant disease) is associated with lower objective_response rate than mcrpc=0.
brca2_mutation=1 is associated with lower objective_response rate than brca2_mutation=0.
ar_v7_positive=1 is associated with lower objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0.
msi_high=1 is associated with lower objective_response rate than msi_high=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.

After adjusting for all baseline features and other treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains positively associated with objective_response while treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab show no significant adjusted main effect on objective_response.
visceral_mets and psma_high have no main effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(df['mcrpc'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: mcrpc=1 response rate 15.4% vs mcrpc=0 rate 34.6% (diff -19.2 pp). chi-square p<<1e-300.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: brca2_mutation=1 response rate 15.0% vs brca2_mutation=0 rate 25.0% (diff -10.1 pp). p=5.4e-56.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on ar_v7_positive
analyses.result_summary: ar_v7_positive=1 response rate 16.0% vs ar_v7_positive=0 rate 26.0% (diff -10.1 pp). p=1.9e-98.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on msi_high
analyses.result_summary: msi_high=1 response rate 17.6% vs msi_high=0 rate 24.2% (diff -6.6 pp). p=3.0e-9.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ ecog_ps)
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic coefficient for ecog_ps = -0.285 per unit (p=9.5e-73). Response 25% (ecog<=1) vs 18% (ecog=2).

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ psa_ng_ml)
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic coefficient for psa_ng_ml = -0.0047 (p=1.6e-94). Response 31.8% in lowest PSA quartile vs 15.3% in highest.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ albumin_g_dl)
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic coefficient for albumin_g_dl = +0.096 per g/dL (p=4.4e-6). Direction: higher albumin → higher response.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ weight_loss_pct_6mo)
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic coefficient for weight_loss_pct_6mo = -0.032 per percent (p=3.4e-29). Higher weight loss → lower response.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ crp_mg_l)
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic coefficient for crp_mg_l = -0.0069 per mg/L (p=5.6e-8). Higher CRP → lower response.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ const + all_features + all_treatments)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted (full) logistic regression: treatment_enzalutamide coef=+1.20 (p<<1e-300); treatment_abiraterone coef=-0.025 (p=0.31); treatment_docetaxel coef=+0.002 (p=0.95); treatment_olaparib coef=+0.026 (p=0.49); treatment_lu177_psma coef=+0.029 (p=0.35); treatment_pembrolizumab coef=+0.014 (p=0.79). visceral_mets coef=+0.002 (p=0.93); psma_high coef=-0.029 (p=0.20). Strong adjusted negative effects: mcrpc -1.13, brca2 -0.74, ar_v7 -0.69, msi -0.43, ecog -0.33, weight_loss -0.036, psa -0.001, crp -0.007. Albumin +0.13.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by mcrpc (smaller benefit in mcrpc=1).
The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by ar_v7_positive (smaller benefit in ar_v7=1).
The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by brca2_mutation (smaller benefit in brca2=1).
The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by msi_high (smaller benefit in msi_high=1).
The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is modified by psa_ng_ml (smaller benefit at higher PSA).
Among the 5 non-enzalutamide treatments, no single feature × treatment interaction reaches genome-wide significance equivalent (e.g., p<1e-4).

analyses.code: For each treatment t and each feature f: Logit(y ~ const + t + f + t*f). Record interaction coef and p.
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature interaction screen (150 tests). Top hits all involve enzalutamide: enza × mcrpc beta=-1.98 p≈0; enza × ar_v7_positive beta=-1.24 p=1.4e-93; enza × psa_ng_ml beta=-0.0087 p=1.2e-72; enza × brca2_mutation beta=-1.14 p=3.3e-42; enza × msi_high beta=-1.28 p=1.8e-19; enza × ecog_ps beta=+0.14 p=1.6e-5. For non-enzalutamide treatments, no interaction reaches p<1e-2 except a few weak signals: lu177 × visceral_mets p=0.010, docetaxel × sodium p=0.023, docetaxel × alt p=0.028, abiraterone × brca2 p=0.031, olaparib × bun p=0.031, pembrolizumab × gleason p=0.033, olaparib × brca2 p=0.045.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab increases objective_response rate within msi_high=1 patients (classical immunotherapy biomarker hypothesis).
Olaparib increases objective_response rate within brca2_mutation=1 patients (classical PARP-inhibitor synthetic-lethality hypothesis).
Lu177-PSMA increases objective_response rate within psma_high=1 patients.
Enzalutamide effect on objective_response is largely abolished in ar_v7_positive=1 patients (AR-V7 resistance hypothesis).
Enzalutamide effect is concentrated in mcrpc=0 patients (hormone-sensitive setting), with little/no effect in mcrpc=1 patients.
Abiraterone has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response within ar_v7_positive=1 or mcrpc=1 patients.
Docetaxel has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response in mcrpc=1 or in visceral_mets=1.

analyses.code: Within msi_high==1: rate_pembro_on - rate_pembro_off; chi-square; adjusted logistic with full covariates and pembro*msi interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Within msi_high=1 (n=1528): pembrolizumab on response 17.7% (n=79) vs off 17.6% (n=1449); diff +0.1 pp; chi2 p=1.0. Adjusted pembro × msi_high interaction = -0.04 (p=0.90). REFUTED — no benefit of pembro in MSI-high subgroup.

analyses.code: Within brca2_mutation==1: rate_olaparib_on - rate_olaparib_off; chi-square; adjusted interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Within brca2_mutation=1 (n=4996): olaparib on response 12.1% (n=529) vs off 15.3% (n=4467); diff -3.2 pp; chi2 p=0.058. Adjusted olaparib × brca2 interaction = -0.29 (p=0.054). REFUTED in expected direction; in fact olaparib trends toward LOWER response within BRCA2-mutated patients.

analyses.code: Within psma_high==1: rate_lu177_on - rate_lu177_off; chi-square; adjusted interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Within psma_high=1 (n=29962): lu177 on 23.8% vs off 23.9%; diff -0.1 pp; chi2 p=0.88. Adjusted lu177 × psma_high interaction = -0.04 (p=0.53). REFUTED — no benefit of lu177-PSMA in PSMA-high subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratify by ar_v7_positive; compute enza on/off response.
analyses.result_summary: ar_v7_positive=1 (n=10038): enza on 16.5% vs off 15.7% (diff +0.8 pp, p=0.27). ar_v7_positive=0 (n=39962): enza on 41.1% vs off 16.0% (diff +25.1 pp, p<<1e-300). SUPPORTED — enzalutamide benefit nearly fully abolished in AR-V7+.

analyses.code: Stratify by mcrpc; compute enza on/off response.
analyses.result_summary: mcrpc=1 (n=27481): enza on 15.8% vs off 15.0% (diff +0.8 pp, p=0.09). mcrpc=0 (n=22519): enza on 61.0% vs off 16.9% (diff +44.0 pp, p<<1e-300). SUPPORTED — enzalutamide benefit overwhelmingly concentrated in mcrpc=0.

analyses.code: Stratify abiraterone effect by ar_v7_positive and by mcrpc.
analyses.result_summary: abi within ar_v7=1: on 16.3% vs off 15.8% (diff +0.5 pp, p=0.55). abi within mcrpc=1: on 15.4% vs off 15.3% (diff +0.1 pp, p=0.89). abi within mcrpc=0: on 33.9% vs off 34.9% (diff -1.0 pp, p=0.17). SUPPORTED — no abiraterone effect in any of these strata.

analyses.code: Stratify docetaxel effect by mcrpc and visceral_mets.
analyses.result_summary: doc within mcrpc=1: on 15.2% vs off 15.4% (diff -0.1 pp, p=0.77). doc within mcrpc=0: on 34.8% vs off 34.5% (diff +0.3 pp, p=0.71). doc within visceral_mets=1: on 24.2% vs off 23.8% (diff +0.4 pp, p=0.68). doc within visceral_mets=0: on 23.9% vs off 24.1% (diff -0.2 pp, p=0.73). SUPPORTED — docetaxel inert in all strata tested.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within mcrpc=0, the four binary biomarkers ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, and msi_high=1 each independently abolish enzalutamide benefit; psma_high and visceral_mets do not modify the enzalutamide effect.
The enzalutamide-responsive subgroup is precisely defined by the conjunction (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0).
Within the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup, ECOG and PSA continue to be prognostic but do not further modify the enzalutamide effect (effect-size remains ≥+60 pp absolute response).

analyses.code: Stratify within mcrpc=0 by each remaining binary feature; compute enza on/off rate.
analyses.result_summary: Within mcrpc=0 (n=22,519): enza diff is +54.6 pp in ar_v7=0 vs +1.4 pp in ar_v7=1; +49.1 pp in brca2=0 vs -1.2 pp in brca2=1; +45.4 pp in msi=0 vs -1.2 pp in msi=1; +45.8 pp in psma_high=0 vs +42.9 pp in psma_high=1 (no effect modification); +44.2 pp in visceral_mets=0 vs +43.2 pp in visceral_mets=1 (no effect modification). SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Subgroup mask = (mcrpc==0) & (ar_v7_positive==0) & (brca2_mutation==0) & (msi_high==0); compute enza on/off response within and outside.
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup IN (n=15,681): enza on 79.8% (n=6,325) vs off 17.2% (n=9,356); diff +62.6 pp (p≈0). Subgroup OUT (n=34,319): enza on 16.1% vs off 15.3%; diff +0.8 pp (p=0.06). Adjusted t × subgroup interaction = +2.94 logit (p≈0); adjusted enzalutamide effect when in subgroup = +3.01 logit. SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Within the enza-responsive subgroup, stratify by PSA quartiles and ECOG.
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup (n=15,681), enza on-vs-off response by PSA quartile: 81.6/18.3 (diff +63.3); 80.4/17.6 (+62.8); 80.3/17.9 (+62.4); 76.8/15.0 (+61.8). By ECOG: 0→85.9/21.3 (+64.6); 1→78.1/15.9 (+62.2); 2→70.9/11.9 (+59.0). SUPPORTED — effect remains ≥+59 pp across all PSA and ECOG strata; PSA and ECOG are prognostic but do not modify the enzalutamide effect within the responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 5 non-enzalutamide treatments, an S-learner gradient-boosting model on (features, treatment) reveals no subgroup with absolute conditional treatment effect (CATE) ≥ 0.05 spanning ≥1,000 patients.

analyses.code: GradientBoostingClassifier(n_estimators=80, max_depth=3) fit to (X, T) -> y for each treatment; CATE = p(y|X,T=1) - p(y|X,T=0).
analyses.result_summary: For abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab: CATE distributions are essentially flat at 0 (min/max within ±0.001). The top-10% CATE subgroup has the same baseline-feature distribution as the full population and shows treatment-vs-control rate differences of -0.003 to +0.003. SUPPORTED — GBM cannot detect any meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity for the non-enzalutamide treatments at the modeled depth.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab increases objective_response rate in the subgroup defined by (albumin_g_dl ≥ median AND crp_mg_l ≤ median) — i.e., patients without inflammation/cachexia.
Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, abiraterone increases objective_response rate (modest magnitude).
Within visceral_mets=1 patients, lu177_psma decreases objective_response rate.
Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, olaparib decreases objective_response rate (opposite of expected biology).

analyses.code: Exhaustive 2-way and 3-way subgroup search over binary features (raw + binarized continuous) for each non-enzalutamide treatment; rank cells by chi-square p-value.
analyses.result_summary: Top hits per treatment: pembrolizumab × (alb_high & crp_low): +6.2 pp (31.5/25.3, p=0.0004); pembrolizumab × (ecog_high=0 & alb_high & crp_low): +6.8 pp (32.9/26.1, p=0.0004). olaparib × brca2_mutation=1 & ecog_high=0: -4.7 pp (11.5/16.2, p=0.013); consistent negative across BRCA2+ slices. lu177-PSMA × visceral_mets=1: -2.4 pp (21.9/24.3, p=0.04); within visceral_mets+ratings, lu177 effect is negative. abiraterone × brca2_mutation=1 (& gleason_high=0 & psa_high=0): +7.4 pp (modest sample). Docetaxel: no consistent subgroup; nominal hits do not survive cross-cut replication.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across continuous features, the pembrolizumab effect is best modified jointly by albumin and CRP rather than by either alone or by other continuous markers.
Within msi_high=1, pembrolizumab still has no benefit even when restricting to inflam_good=1.
Within the enzalutamide-non-responsive subgroup (mcrpc=1 OR ar_v7=1 OR brca2=1 OR msi=1), pembrolizumab continues to provide a benefit when inflam_good=1.

analyses.code: Adjusted logistic with pembro main, inflam_good main, and pembro×inflam_good, plus pembro×albumin and pembro×crp continuous interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted (full covariates): pembrolizumab × inflam_good interaction = +0.44 logit (p=1.1e-4); pembro × albumin (continuous) = -0.012 (p=0.93); pembro × crp (continuous) = +0.0019 (p=0.81). Single-marker pembro × albumin (binary alb_high alone) interaction is small (+0.18 p=0.07); pembro × crp (binary crp_low alone) similarly modest. The conjunction is clearly the relevant modifier. SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Within msi_high==1, compute pembro on/off response.
analyses.result_summary: msi_high=1 (n=1528): pembro on 17.7% vs off 17.6% (diff +0.1 pp, p=1.0). SUPPORTED — even within the immunotherapy-relevant biomarker subgroup, pembrolizumab is inert here.

analyses.code: Restrict to non-enza-responsive set and condition on inflam_good=1; compute pembro on/off response.
analyses.result_summary: Non-resp subgroup AND inflam_good=1 (n=9475): pembro on 22.3% (n=458) vs off 16.8% (n=9017); diff +5.4 pp. SUPPORTED — pembro effect partially preserved even outside the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Both predicates of the pembrolizumab-responsive subgroup are necessary: alb_high alone (with crp high) does NOT yield positive pembro effect; crp_low alone (with alb low) does NOT yield positive pembro effect.
Within the pembrolizumab-responsive subgroup, PSMA, ECOG, AR-V7, mCRPC, BRCA2, MSI, and visceral mets do not appreciably modify the pembrolizumab effect (it is reasonably uniform across substrata).

analyses.code: Compute pembro effect in 4 cells: alb_high&crp_low; alb_high&crp_high; alb_low&crp_low; alb_low&crp_high.
analyses.result_summary: alb_high & crp_low (inflam_good=1): pembro diff +6.2 pp. alb_high only (alb_high & crp_high): diff -2.3 pp. crp_low only (alb_low & crp_low): diff -2.9 pp. both bad: diff -2.8 pp. SUPPORTED — only the joint condition produces a positive pembro effect.

analyses.code: Within inflam_good==1, stratify pembro effect by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2, ar_v7, psma_high, ecog.
analyses.result_summary: Within inflam_good=1 (n=13,678) the pembro on-vs-off difference is: mcrpc=0 +8.1 pp; mcrpc=1 +3.5 pp; visceral_mets=0 +6.7; visceral_mets=1 +4.4; brca2=0 +5.4; brca2=1 +13.0; ar_v7=0 +6.0; ar_v7=1 +7.7; psma_high=0 +6.6; psma_high=1 +5.9; ecog=0 +4.6; ecog=1 +8.2; ecog=2 +2.6. SUPPORTED — effect is consistently positive (range +2.6 to +13.0 pp) across all substrata; no single substratum drives the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Docetaxel has no significant adjusted treatment-by-feature interaction with any of: visceral_mets, mcrpc, ecog_ps, psa_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score.

Lu177-PSMA × visceral_mets interaction is statistically significant in the negative direction in adjusted logistic regression (lu177 effect is positive in visceral_mets=0 and negative in visceral_mets=1).
Abiraterone × brca2_mutation interaction is positive (modest) in adjusted logistic regression — abiraterone effect is more positive in BRCA2 mutated patients than in BRCA2 wild type.
Adjusted treatment effects of abiraterone and docetaxel are both essentially zero with no clinically meaningful subgroup of >1000 patients showing a 5+ pp benefit.

analyses.code: For docetaxel × {visceral_mets, mcrpc, ecog_2, ecog_0, psa_high_med, ldh_high, alp_high, gleason_high}: adjusted logistic with all features + interaction term.
analyses.result_summary: All adjusted docetaxel × feature interactions p > 0.15 (best p=0.15 for alp_high). t_main coefficients all in ±0.05 logit. SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Adjusted logistic: y ~ all_features + all_treatments + lu177*visceral_mets.
analyses.result_summary: lu177 t_main=+0.070 (p=0.045); lu177 × visceral_mets interaction=-0.206 (p=0.0098); lu177 effect when visceral_mets=1 = -0.135. Stratified rates: visceral_mets=0 lu177 on 24.9% vs off 23.9% (diff +1.0 pp p=0.10); visceral_mets=1 lu177 on 21.9% vs off 24.3% (diff -2.4 pp p=0.04). SUPPORTED but small.

analyses.code: Adjusted logistic: y ~ all_features + all_treatments + abiraterone*brca2_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: abiraterone t_main=-0.039 (p=0.13); abi × brca2 interaction=+0.186 (p=0.045); abi effect when brca2=1 = +0.147. Stratified rates: brca2=0 abi on 24.7% vs off 25.2% (diff -0.5 pp p=0.23); brca2=1 abi on 16.4% vs off 14.3% (diff +2.1 pp p=0.06). SUPPORTED, modest magnitude.

analyses.code: Adjusted (full feature + full treatment) logistic regression coefficients for abiraterone and docetaxel.
analyses.result_summary: abiraterone adj coef=-0.025 (p=0.31); docetaxel adj coef=+0.002 (p=0.95). Neither has any subgroup of n>1000 with on-vs-off rate difference exceeding ~5 pp robust across cross-cuts. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: enzalutamide is responsive only in the joint subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0); each of the four predicates is necessary, and the four together are sufficient for a +60+ pp absolute response benefit.

FINAL: pembrolizumab is responsive only in the joint subgroup (albumin_g_dl ≥ population median 3.8 AND crp_mg_l ≤ population median 3.34); both predicates are necessary; the effect is +6 pp absolute with adjusted t × subgroup interaction p~1e-4.
FINAL: olaparib's adjusted effect is approximately zero in the overall cohort and weakly NEGATIVE in BRCA2-mutated patients (interaction p=0.054); it has no responsive subgroup with positive effect.

FINAL: lu177-PSMA's adjusted effect is approximately zero in the overall cohort and weakly NEGATIVE in visceral-mets+ patients (interaction p=0.01); it has no responsive subgroup with clinically meaningful positive effect.
FINAL: abiraterone has approximately zero overall effect; a small positive effect in BRCA2+ (interaction p=0.045) is the only signal — too small to define a clinically meaningful responsive subgroup.
FINAL: docetaxel has approximately zero overall effect and no responsive subgroup detectable from the available features.

analyses.code: Necessity check: drop each of the 4 predicates from the conjunction and recompute enza on/off rate within (others-pass + dropped-condition-violated).
analyses.result_summary: Drop mcrpc=0 (so mcrpc=1, ar_v7=0, brca2=0, msi=0): n=19,163, enza diff +0.7 pp. Drop ar_v7=0 (mcrpc=0, ar_v7=1, brca2=0, msi=0): n=3,940, diff +2.2 pp. Drop brca2=0 (mcrpc=0, ar_v7=0, brca2=1, msi=0): n=1,775, diff +0.4 pp. Drop msi=0 (mcrpc=0, ar_v7=0, brca2=0, msi=1): n=494, diff -2.5 pp. SUPPORTED — each predicate is necessary; removing any single one collapses the +63 pp benefit to ≤2 pp.

analyses.code: Subgroup mask = (albumin>=3.8) & (crp<=3.34); chi-square within and outside; adjusted t × subgroup interaction.
analyses.result_summary: IN (n=13,678): pembro on 31.5% (n=670) vs off 25.3% (n=13,008), diff +6.2 pp, p=4.4e-4. OUT (n=36,322): pembro on 20.9% vs off 23.5%, diff -2.7 pp, p=0.012. Adjusted t × subgroup interaction = +0.44 (p=1.1e-4); adjusted pembro effect when in subgroup = +0.32 logit. SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Adjusted olaparib × brca2 logistic; stratified rates within brca2=1.
analyses.result_summary: brca2=1 (n=4996): olaparib on 12.1% vs off 15.3%, diff -3.2 pp (p=0.058); adjusted interaction -0.29 (p=0.054). brca2=0: olaparib effect ~0. SUPPORTED — no positive responsive subgroup; weak negative trend in BRCA2+.

analyses.code: Adjusted lu177 × visceral_mets logistic; stratified rates.
analyses.result_summary: visceral_mets=0: lu177 on 24.9 vs off 23.9 (diff +1.0 pp p=0.10). visceral_mets=1: lu177 on 21.9 vs off 24.3 (diff -2.4 pp p=0.043). Adjusted interaction -0.206 (p=0.0098). SUPPORTED — small negative effect in visceral-mets+, no positive responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: Adjusted abiraterone × brca2 logistic; stratified rates.
analyses.result_summary: brca2=0: abi on 24.7 vs off 25.2 (diff -0.5 pp p=0.23). brca2=1: abi on 16.4 vs off 14.3 (diff +2.1 pp p=0.062). Adjusted interaction +0.186 (p=0.045). Effect magnitude small. SUPPORTED but not clinically meaningful.

analyses.code: Adjusted docetaxel coefficient and all docetaxel × feature interactions tested.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted docetaxel coef=+0.002 (p=0.95). All adjusted docetaxel × feature interactions p>0.15. Stratified by mcrpc, visceral_mets, ecog, psa, ldh, alp, gleason: no cell with diff exceeding ±5 pp at n>1000 with cross-cut replication. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: All patients in this cohort have sex_female=0 (the cohort is exclusively male, as expected for prostate cancer).

After accounting for the four binary modifiers of enzalutamide (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high), the residual prognostic value of psa_ng_ml within the responsive subgroup is small (across PSA quartiles, the on-vs-off difference varies by <5 pp) — i.e., PSA is prognostic but not predictive for enzalutamide within this subgroup.
Within the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup, none of the other 5 treatments adds incremental response benefit (each shows on-vs-off difference within ±2 pp on top of enzalutamide use).

analyses.code: df['sex_female'].value_counts()
analyses.result_summary: sex_female is 0 for all 50,000 patients (mean=0, std=0). SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Within (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7=0 & brca2=0 & msi=0), stratify by PSA quartile; compute enza on/off rate.
analyses.result_summary: PSA q1 (low): on 81.6/off 18.3, diff +63.3 pp. q2: 80.4/17.6, +62.8. q3: 80.3/17.9, +62.4. q4 (high): 76.8/15.0, +61.8. Range across quartiles: 1.5 pp. SUPPORTED — PSA does not modify the enzalutamide effect within the responsive subgroup despite being prognostic for raw response rate.

analyses.code: Within (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7=0 & brca2=0 & msi=0), compute on/off response for each non-enzalutamide treatment.
analyses.result_summary: abiraterone diff -1.2 pp; docetaxel +0.5 pp; olaparib -0.1 pp; lu177-PSMA -0.7 pp; pembrolizumab -1.7 pp. SUPPORTED — none of the other 5 treatments adds detectable incremental benefit in this subgroup.
